title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses low direct risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by malicious actors to identify and exploit vulnerabilities in unpatched systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure it aligns with your organization's security policies.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool itself to mitigate any potential vulnerabilities within the scanner.
Educate teams on the responsible use of such tools to prevent accidental exposure of sensitive information.

title: Incus is affected by unbounded binary import disk exhaustion
description:
### Summary
Uploads of large amount of data by authenticated users can run the Incus server out of disk space, potentially taking down the host system.

The impact here is limited for anyone using `storage.images_volume` and `storage.backups_volume` as those users will have large uploads be stored on those volumes rather than directly on the host filesystem. This is the default behavior on IncusOS.

### Details
Multiple binary import paths accept application/octet-stream requests and stream the

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to upload large amounts of data, potentially exhausting disk space on the host system. This could lead to denial-of-service (DoS) conditions, disrupting operations and impacting system availability.
recommended_actions: Implement strict disk quota limits for authenticated users to prevent excessive disk usage.
Ensure that `storage.images_volume` and `storage.backups_volume` are configured and used as intended to isolate large uploads from the host filesystem.
Apply the latest security patches or updates from Incus to address CVE-2026-41685.

title: Incus has Nil Dereferences on Restore via Malformed YAML
description:
### Summary

### Details
It was found that backup.GetInfo() trusts the inline backup/index.yaml config when present and only falls back to parsing the legacy backup/container/backup.yaml file if result.Config == nil. As a result, an archive can carry a valid inline config that passes the initial import preflight while also carrying a malformed legacy backup/container/backup.yaml file that is reparsed later from the restored file system.

ParseConfigYamlFile() accepts YAML documents with no conta

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41684.

title: Incus has Unbounded YAML Metadata Decode via Parsing
description:
### Summary
User provided image and backup tarballs would be unpacked and YAML files parsed without any size restrictions. This was making it easy for an authenticated user to provide a crafted image or backup tarball that when parsed by Incus would lead to a very large YAML document being loaded into memory, potentially causing the entire server to run out of memory.

### Details
It was found that getImageMetadata and backup.GetInfo call yaml.NewDecoder(tr).Decode() directly on the tar reader w

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41648.

title: Incus has Nil-Pointer Dereference via S3 Bucket Import
description:
### Summary
Missing error handling could lead an authenticated Incus user to cause a daemon crash through the import of a truncated storage bucket backup file.

### Details
It was found that TransferManager.UploadAllFiles iterates over tar entries but only checks for io.EOF from tr.Next(). When tr.Next() returns a non-EOF error, such as unexpected EOF from a truncated archive, the header hdr is nil and the code continues to access hdr.Name, causing a nil-pointer dereference that panics the daemo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41647.

title: Kata Container has CopyFile Policy Subversion via Symlinks
description:
### Summary
An oversight in the CopyFile policy (and perhaps the CopyFile handler) allows untrusted hosts to write to arbitrary locations inside the guest workload image. This can be used to overwrite binaries inside the guest and exfiltrate data from containers; even those running inside CVMs.

### Details
Here is the policy that covers CopyFile requests.
```
CopyFileRequest if {
    print("CopyFileRequest: input.path =", input.path)
        
    check_directory_traversal(input.path)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Kata Containers' CopyFile policy allows attackers to exploit symlink traversal, enabling unauthorized writes to arbitrary locations within the guest workload image. This could lead to the compromise of containerized workloads, data exfiltration, and potential execution of malicious binaries.
recommended_actions: Patch and update all affected Kata Container versions to the latest release that addresses CVE-2026-41326.
Implement additional validation checks for symlinks and directory traversal in CopyFile requests to prevent exploitation.
Monitor container workloads for unusual file modifications or binary changes that may indicate compromise.

title: OpenMRS has Stored Velocity SSTI to RCE via ConceptReferenceRange
description:
### Impact

The `ConceptReferenceRangeUtility.evaluateCriteria()` method in OpenMRS Core
evaluates database-stored criteria strings as Apache Velocity templates without any sandbox configuration. The `VelocityEngine` is initialized with only logging properties and no`SecureUberspector`, leaving the default `UberspectImpl` in place, which allows
unrestricted Java reflection through template expressions.

A user with the `Manage Concepts` privilege can store a malicious Velocity template
expressio

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenMRS Core allows attackers with 'Manage Concepts' privilege to execute arbitrary code on the server via stored Velocity Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). This could lead to full system compromise, data breaches, and potential lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update OpenMRS Core to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-41258.
Restrict access to the 'Manage Concepts' privilege to only the most trusted and essential personnel.
Implement input validation and sanitization for all user inputs, especially those stored as templates, to prevent malicious code execution.

title: Traefik's errors middleware forwards Authorization and Cookie headers to separate error page service
description:
## Summary

There is a medium severity information disclosure vulnerability in Traefik's `errors` (custom error pages) middleware. When the backend returns a response matching the configured status range, the middleware forwards the original request's complete header set, including `Authorization`, `Cookie`, and other authentication material, to the separate error page service rather than only the minimal context needed to render the error page. This behavior is undocumented: the documentation s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41181.

title: Gotenberg has an ExifTool Dangerous Tag Blocklist Bypass via Group-Prefixed Tag Names that Allows Arbitrary File Rename and Move
description:
### Summary

Gotenberg blocks certain ExifTool tag names like `FileName` and `Directory` to stop attackers from renaming or moving files on the server. But ExifTool allows a longer form of the same tag — `System:FileName` — which does the exact same thing. Gotenberg only checks if the tag is exactly `FileName`, so `System:FileName` slips right through and ExifTool happily renames the file. No login is needed. One HTTP request is enough.

This bypasses the fix from [GHSA-qmwh-9m9c-h36m](https://g

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40893.

title: Incus Vulnerable to Panic via Snapshot Bounds Check
description:
### Summary
Missing validation logic in the storage volume import logic allows an authenticated user with access to Incus' storage volume feature to cause the Incus daemon to crash. Repeated use of this issue can be used to keep Incus offline causing a denial of service.

### Details
The backup restore subsystem contains an out-of-bounds panic vulnerability caused by an invalid bounds check when indexing snapshot metadata arrays. The same flawed pattern also appears in the migration path.

When

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40251.

title: Incus has an OVN TLS Verification that Accepts Peer-Supplied Roots
description:
### Summary
Broken TLS validation logic in the OVN database connection logic could allow connections to an attacker's OVN database.

OVN uses mTLS for authentication, so the attacker cannot actually perform a full man in the middle attack as they won't be able to authenticated with the real OVN deployment. At best they can provide a replacement empty database which Incus will briefly interact with before hitting errors due to the rest of the OVN stack not reacting to the committed changes.

Also

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OVN TLS verification could allow an attacker to connect to an OVN database using a peer-supplied root, potentially leading to limited interactions with a replacement empty database. However, due to mTLS authentication, the attacker cannot fully exploit this for a man-in-the-middle attack or make significant changes to the real OVN deployment.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of the software where the vulnerability is patched.
Implement strict monitoring and logging of OVN database connections to detect any unauthorized access attempts.
Ensure proper configuration of mTLS certificates and regularly audit certificate trust chains.

title: Arelle before 2.39.10 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the ...
description: Arelle before 2.39.10 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the /rest/configure REST endpoint that accepts a plugins query parameter and forwards it to the plugin manager without authentication or authorization. Attackers can supply a URL to a malicious Python file through the plugins parameter, causing the Arelle webserver to download and execute the attacker-controlled code within the Arelle process with its privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on the Arelle webserver, potentially leading to full system compromise, data theft, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Arelle to version 2.39.10 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the /rest/configure endpoint using network-level controls such as firewalls or IP whitelisting.
Conduct a security audit to identify any signs of exploitation and ensure no malicious code has been executed on the server.

title: Detect-It-Easy prior to 3.21 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows attackers to...
description: Detect-It-Easy prior to 3.21 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows attackers to write arbitrary files to the filesystem by crafting malicious archive entries with relative traversal sequences or absolute paths. Attackers can exploit insufficient path normalization during archive extraction to write files outside the intended extraction directory and achieve persistent code execution by overwriting user startup scripts.

description: BusyBox before commit 42202bf contains a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the DHCPv6 client (udhcpc6) DNS_SERVERS option handler in networking/udhcp/d6_dhcpc.c that allows network-adjacent attackers to trigger memory corruption by sending a crafted DHCPv6 response with a malformed D6_OPT_DNS_SERVERS option. Attackers can exploit incorrect heap buffer allocation calculations in the option_to_env() function to cause denial of service or achieve arbitrary code execution on embedded systems wit

title:
In Apache Iceberg, the table's metadata files are control files: they tell readers
which data...
description:
In Apache Iceberg, the table's metadata files are control files: they tell readers
which data files belong to the table and which table version to read.



`write.metadata.path` is an optional table property that tells Polaris
where to
write those metadata files. 
For a table already registered in a
Polaris-managed
catalog, changing only that property through an `ALTER TABLE`-style settings
change (not a row-level `INSERT`, `SELECT`, `UPDATE`, or `DELETE`) bypasses
the commit-time branch that is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42812.

title: D-Link DIR-456U Hardware Revision A1 (End-of-Life, EOL) contains a hardcoded telnet backdoor. The...
description: D-Link DIR-456U Hardware Revision A1 (End-of-Life, EOL) contains a hardcoded telnet backdoor. The device starts a telnet daemon at boot via /etc/init0.d/S80telnetd.sh with the username "Alphanetworks" and the static password "whdrv01_dlob_dir456U" read from /etc/config/image_sign. The custom telnetd binary accepts a -u user:password flag, and the custom login binary uses strcmp() to validate credentials. Successful authentication grants an unauthenticated attacker on the local network a root she

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The hardcoded telnet backdoor in the D-Link DIR-456U Hardware Revision A1 allows unauthenticated attackers on the local network to gain root access to the device. This can lead to full device compromise, enabling attackers to manipulate network traffic, exfiltrate sensitive data, or use the device as a pivot point for further attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately replace or decommission the affected D-Link DIR-456U devices, as they are End-of-Life (EOL) and no longer receive security updates.
Segment the network to isolate vulnerable devices and restrict access to the local network to trusted users only.
Implement network monitoring to detect and block unauthorized telnet traffic and other suspicious activities.

title:
OOM Denial of Service via Unbounded Array Allocation in Apache OpenNLP AbstractModelReader 
...
description:
OOM Denial of Service via Unbounded Array Allocation in Apache OpenNLP AbstractModelReader 

Versions Affected: 

before 2.5.9

Description:


The AbstractModelReader methods getOutcomes(), getOutcomePatterns(), and getPredicates() each read a 32-bit signed integer count field from a binary model stream and pass that value directly to an array allocation (new String[numOutcomes], new int[numOCTypes][], new String[NUM_PREDS]) without validating that the value is non-negative or

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42440.

title:
In plain terms, Apache Polaris is supposed to issue short-lived GCS credentials
that
only work...
description:
In plain terms, Apache Polaris is supposed to issue short-lived GCS credentials
that
only work for one table's files, but a crafted namespace or table name can
cause those credentials to work across the configured bucket instead.


Apache Polaris builds Google Cloud Storage downscoped credentials by creating a
Credential Access Boundary (CAB) with CEL conditions that are intended to
restrict access to the requested table's storage path.



The relevant CEL string is built from the bucket name an

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to bypass intended access restrictions in Apache Polaris by crafting specific namespace or table names. As a result, short-lived Google Cloud Storage (GCS) credentials can be escalated to access the entire configured bucket instead of being limited to a specific table's files. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exposure of sensitive information stored in GCS.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Apache Polaris to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-42811.
Review and audit all existing GCS bucket configurations and access policies to ensure no unauthorized access has occurred.
Implement additional monitoring and logging for GCS access to detect any anomalous or unauthorized activity.

title: Easy PayPal Events & Tickets plugin for WordPress version 1.3 and earlier contain a hardcoded...
description: Easy PayPal Events & Tickets plugin for WordPress version 1.3 and earlier contain a hardcoded authentication bypass vulnerability in the QR code scanning functionality that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass hash verification by supplying 'test' as the hash parameter. Attackers can access the vulnerable endpoint via the add_wpeevent_button_qr action to retrieve sensitive order details including PayPal transaction IDs, customer email addresses, purchase amounts, and ticket informat

title:
Apache Polaris accepts literal `*` characters in namespace and table names. When it
later builds...
description:
Apache Polaris accepts literal `*` characters in namespace and table names. When it
later builds temporary S3 access policies for delegated table access, those
same characters appear to be reused unescaped in S3 IAM resource patterns
and
`s3:prefix` conditions.



In S3 IAM policy matching, `*` is treated as a wildcard rather than as
ordinary text. That means temporary credentials issued for one crafted table
can match the storage path of a different table.



In private testing against Polaris

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in Apache Polaris allows attackers to exploit the mishandling of `*` characters in namespace and table names, leading to unauthorized access to S3 storage paths. By crafting specific table names, attackers can gain access to data stored in other tables, potentially exposing sensitive information or enabling data tampering.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any patches or updates provided by the Apache Polaris team to address CVE-2026-42810.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for namespace and table names to prevent the use of special characters like `*`.
Review and audit S3 IAM policies to ensure that wildcard characters are not unintentionally granting excessive permissions.

title: CVE-2026-7076 - A vulnerability was determined in itsourcecode Courier Management System 1.0. Impacted is an unknown
description: A vulnerability was determined in itsourcecode Courier Management System 1.0. Impacted is an unknown function of the file /edit_branch.php. Executing a manipulation of the argument ID can lead to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit a SQL injection flaw in the Courier Management System 1.0, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, or database manipulation. The exploit is remotely executable and publicly disclosed, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to mitigate SQL injection vulnerabilities in the affected application.
Update or patch the Courier Management System to the latest version if a fix is available from the vendor.
Implement web application firewalls (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts as an interim measure.

title: CVE-2026-42363 - An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVis
description:
An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5. Listening to broadcast packets can lead to credentials leak. An attacker can listen to broadcast messages to trigger this vulnerability.


When interacting with various Geovision devices on the network, the utility may send privileged commands; in order to do so, the username and password of the device need to be provided. In some instances the command is broadcasted over UDP and the username/password are encrypted using a cryptographic protocol that appears to be derivated from Blowfish. However the symmetric key used for the encryption is also included in the packet, and thus the security of the username/password only relies on the "obscurity" of the encryption scheme. An attacker on the same LAN can listen to the broadcast traffic once an admin user interacts with the device, and decrypt the credentials using their own implementation of the algorithm. With this password the attacker would have full control over the device configuration, allowing them to change its ip address or even reset it to factory default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42363.
